summary: Full E2E test of Midget jr. (FastAPI + MongoDB backend + React frontend). All 11 pytest backend tests passed and all critical frontend flows (lock/unlock, all 6 tabs render, chat reply, queue add, file import) succeeded.

ui_bugs.issue: On wrong-password, the error text shown to the user is the misleading raw JS message 'Failed to execute text on Response: body stream already read' instead of 'Wrong password'. Likely caused by double-reading res.text() somewhere (possibly React 18 StrictMode double-invoke of fetch in dev). The unlock itself correctly rejects wrong passwords (401), but the UX error string is wrong.

action_items: (Optional) Fix wrong-password error message in PasswordModal: in api() helper, read res.text() into a variable before any parse and return data.detail; ensure no double await on res. Currently the user sees a confusing 'body stream already read' string instead of 'Wrong password' on 401.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 528-line file — acceptable for this MVP but consider splitting routers/services if it grows.

/api/chat sets session_id='chat-session' as a constant — all callers share the same LlmChat session on the server side. With multiple users this could leak context across users. Fine for a single-admin self-hosted app, but worth noting.
/api/queue (GET) and /api/knowledge (GET) are not admin-protected — by design (frontend reads them while locked). Fine, but be aware that knowledge content is publicly readable.
do_research mutates the inserted dict with Mongo's _id and re-uses it; explicit `dict(entry)` copy is used so this is safe — good.
Chat history replay loop on line 326-332 is dead code (pass-only); the actual recap is built below via the recap string. Cleanup recommended but not blocking.

Frontend api() helper does res.text() once then JSON.parse — should be safe, but the wrong-password modal sometimes shows a 'body stream already read' message, suggesting some other layer is also reading the body. Worth investigating.
test_credentials: Admin password: MidgetsRcool (from /app/backend/.env ADMIN_PASSWORD). /app/memory/test_credentials.md was not present — relied on the password provided in the review request.
seed_data_creation: Tests insert TEST_ prefixed knowledge entries and queue items. Queue items are deleted by the test itself. KB entries with the random 'zorblax<hex>' topic remain in the DB (used to validate context_used > 0 in chat). Volume is small (~1 entry per run).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_midgetjr.py exercise unlock (right+wrong pw), root, chat, code, import (auth-gated and unauth), query, chat-with-KB-context, research, and full queue CRUD with auth gates. Run: pytest backend/tests/test_midgetjr.py -v. Frontend tested via Playwright covering lock toggle, password modal, all 6 tab render, chat send/reply, queue add, file import. Test users: only the single admin password 'MidgetsRcool'. Note research test depends on DDG fallback availability — if DDG also blocks, sources_found may be 0 (which is still a 200 — assertion only checks shape).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. Only one cosmetic UX issue on the wrong-password error string.
